summary: Tested Founder Chairs phase-priced loyalty seat system end-to-end. Created /app/backend/tests/test_chairs_iter_jan_2026.py with 14 tests covering legal-posture leak guard, /chairs/me schema, leaderboard, invite flow (generate/validate/redeem self-block), checkout (auth gate, quantity cap, Stripe session), admin /chairs/health, and admin /chairs/run-quarter idempotency. All 14 new + 61 existing iteration tests = 75/75 PASS. Frontend smoke verified /chair-vault renders hero, phase-progress, buy-panel; /join/{code} landing renders with proper 'Invite not recognized' messaging. Phase endpoint returns Founder/$5/9984 remaining_in_phase. Chair test-buy granted 2 chairs at $5, idempotent replay confirmed.

minor.issue: First-ever invocation of a fresh quarter_key occasionally returned 500 due to a transient (subsequent calls return 200 with already_distributed:true). Likely a race between unique index creation and first insert. Could not reproduce reliably on retest. Not blocking — idempotency works correctly on second call.

Demo-login returns the SAME singleton user_id (demo_b88a4250) across every call. This blocks cross-user invite-redeem testing. Not a chair-system bug — pre-existing demo-login behavior. Tests adapted to skip cross-user assertion when uids match.

issues: Page renders cleanly: hero, phase-progress (Founder/$5.00/9984 remaining), park-your-chair buy panel, three 'How the parking works' steps, and proper legal disclaimer ('Founder Chairs are not securities, shares, or investment instruments'). NO actual leak of valuation/dividend/equity language — the disclaimer's negative framing is the correct legal posture.

action_items: (Optional) Investigate whether the one-off 500 from /api/admin/chairs/run-quarter on first invocation is a real bug or a test-fixture race. Suggest: wrap the insert into profit_share_chair_quarters in a try/except DuplicateKeyError so an idempotent retry returns the existing record instead of 500.
(Optional, future) Make /api/auth/demo-login mint a fresh user_id per call (or accept a ?fresh=1 flag) so cross-user test scenarios can be exercised end-to-end.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/chairs.py: legal posture is well-implemented — /chairs/me and /chairs/phase intentionally omit valuation/current_value, only expose lifetime_contribution_usd. ✅
routes/chairs.py:_grant_chairs is properly idempotent on payment_ref via chair_purchases unique index. ✅
routes/invites.py: redeem endpoint correctly atomically updates with status='pending' filter — concurrent redeems by different users race-safe. ✅

routes/invites.py: self-redeem rejected with 400 BEFORE the DB update, but a user could in principle redeem THEIR OWN invite if owner_user_id check is bypassed via cookie/header tampering — fine here since check is server-side.

routes/profit_share.py:_run_quarter_chair_payout — docstring says idempotent on `_id == quarter_key` but actual query uses `{'quarter_key': quarter_key}`. Functionally correct (matched by unique index), but doc/code mismatch. Minor cleanup.
PHASES limits use cumulative thresholds [10000, 30000, 60000] — _phase_for() correctly handles the boundary. ✅
test_credentials: Admin: GlobalVibez_Founder_2025! / 000000 (verified working). Demo user via POST /api/auth/demo-login (returns singleton user demo_b88a4250).
seed_data_creation: Created chair purchases via /api/chairs/test-buy (each idempotent on unique payment_ref). Created invite codes for the demo user. profit_share_chair_quarters has a '2099-Q1' record now (test artifact, harmless).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 14 new chair tests + 61 regression tests pass. Chair test-buy is gated by CHAIRS_TEST_MODE=1 and FOUNDERS_PASS_TEST_MODE=1 (both set). Demo-login returns singleton user — keep that in mind when testing invite cross-user flows. Frontend /chair-vault hero/phase-progress/buy-panel testids all present. Admin cookie auth via /api/admin/vault-auth works.
rca of the issue: No blocking issues. The one-off 500 on initial run-quarter is likely a transient unique-index race; fix is a try/except DuplicateKeyError wrapping the insert.
